meaning: in or to a foreign country
used to emphasize that something is completely true
at a later time; after an event that has already been mentioned
examples: Afterwards she was sorry for what she'd said.

meaning: one more time; on another occasion
used in expressions of time with the simple past tense to show how far in the past something happened
further forward in space or time; in front
examples: I'll run ahead and warn them.
She's fluent in French and German. She also speaks a little Italian.

meaning: often used at the end of negative sentences and at the end of questions, to mean ‚Äėany longer‚Äô
examples: She doesn't live here any more.

meaning: used when adding something to support an idea or argument
examples: It's too expensive and anyway the colour doesn't suit you.

meaning: separated by a distance, of space or time
examples: The two houses stood 500 metres apart.

meaning: according to what you have heard or read; according to the way something appears
examples: Apparently they are getting divorced soon.

meaning: used to show that something is almost, but not completely, accurate or correct
examples: The journey took approximately seven hours.

meaning: towards a place or position that is behind
examples: I lost my balance and fell backwards.

meaning: in the most important ways, without considering things that are less important
examples: I think we are basically saying the same thing.
He walked into the room, closely followed by the rest of the family.

meaning: usually; very often; by most people
examples: Christopher is commonly known as Kit.

meaning: in every way possible; in every part
examples: It was completely different from how I had imagined it.

meaning: in a way that is accurate or true, without any mistakes
a way of emphasizing that something is true and that there is no doubt about it
examples: I definitely remember sending the letter.

meaning: done in a way that was planned, not by chance
examples: She's been deliberately ignoring him all day.

meaning: in a different way from somebody/something
examples: Boys and girls may behave differently.
The path leads directly to the river.

meaning: towards the ground; towards a lower place or position
examples: She was lying face downwards on the grass.

meaning: in a way that produces the intended result or a successful result
examples: The company must reduce costs to compete effectively.

meaning: in addition to something already mentioned
in, at or to another place or other places

examples: The experience was entirely new to me.

meaning: to the same degree; in the same or in a similar way
examples: Diet and exercise are equally important.

meaning: more with one person, thing, etc. than with others, or more in particular circumstances than in others
examples: Teenagers are very fashion conscious, especially girls.

meaning: at the end of a period of time or a series of events
examples: Our flight eventually left five hours late.

meaning: used in negative sentences and questions, or sentences with if to mean ‚Äėat any time‚Äô
used to emphasize that something is correct in every way or in every detail

examples: It is extremely important to follow the directions exactly.

meaning: to some extent but not very
used to introduce the first of a list of points you want to make in a speech or piece of writing
examples: There are two reasons for this decision: firstly‚Ä¶

meaning: used to say that a particular situation or state will always exist

examples: I was late, but fortunately the meeting hadn't started.
Buses run frequently between the city and the airport.

meaning: in addition to what has just been stated. Furthermore is used especially to add a point to an argument.
examples: He said he had not discussed the matter with her. Furthermore, he had not even contacted her.
The initiative was generally considered a success.

meaning: slowly, over a long period of time
almost no; almost not; almost none
to a great degree; in large amounts

examples: It is highly unlikely that she'll be late.

meaning: more and more all the time
examples: Tourism is playing an increasingly important role in the region's economy

meaning: used after very and an adjective or adverb to emphasize a statement, description, etc.

examples: I prefer to stay indoors in this really hot weather.
Lee was ill so I went instead.

meaning: to a great extent; mostly or mainly
examples: The manager was largely responsible for the team‚Äôs victory.

meaning: more than anything else; also used to talk about the most important reason for something
examples: They eat mainly fruit and nuts.

meaning: used when you are not certain that something will happen or that something is true or is a correct number
examples: Maybe he'll come, maybe he won't.
Leave the cake to cool completely. Meanwhile, make the topping.

meaning: in a way that you would expect
examples: Naturally, I get upset when things go wrong.

meaning: used to say that something cannot be avoided
examples: The number of places available is necessarily limited.

meaning: despite something that you have just mentioned
examples: There is little chance that we will succeed in changing the law. Nevertheless, it is important that we try.
I would never normally discuss this.

meaning: used with be, do or have to form the negative of verbs; used to form the negative of modal verbs like can or must
used with the numbers 1 to 12 when telling the time, to mean an exact hour
examples: He left between five and six o'clock.

meaning: used when giving information that you expect other people to know already or agree with
examples: Obviously, we don't want to spend too much money.
We occasionally meet for a drink after work.

meaning: used to describe the situation that existed at the beginning of a particular period or activity, especially before something was changed
examples: The school was originally very small.

meaning: used to state what the result would be if something did not happen or if the situation were different
examples: My parents lent me the money. Otherwise, I couldn't have afforded the trip.

meaning: away from the inside of a place or thing
examples: She ran out into the corridor.

meaning: outside, rather than in a building
examples: The rain prevented them from eating outdoors.

meaning: especially; more than usual or more than others

examples: Some people are unwilling to attend the classes partly because of the cost involved.
It's perfectly normal to feel like this.

meaning: used to show that you are giving your own opinion about something
examples: Personally, I prefer the second option.

meaning: used to say that something might exist, happen or be true, but you are not certain
examples: It was possibly their worst performance ever.

meaning: at a time before the time that you are talking about
examples: The building had previously been used as a hotel.

meaning: in a way that is correct and/or appropriate
examples: How much money do we need to do the job properly?

meaning: very quickly; at a great rate

examples: She is rarely seen in public nowadays.

meaning: used to mean ‚Äėfairly‚Äô or ‚Äėto some degree‚Äô, often when you are disappointed, surprised or expressing slight criticism

examples: We meet regularly to discuss the progress of the project.

meaning: to a fairly large degree, especially in comparison to something else
used to introduce the second of a list of points you want to make in a speech or piece of writing
examples: Firstly, it's expensive, and secondly, it's too slow.

meaning: in a way that is large or important enough to have an effect on something or to be noticed

examples: It is a little cheaper than other similarly sized cars.

meaning: used to emphasize how easy or basic something is
connected with or intended for one particular thing only
examples: liquid vitamins specifically designed for children

meaning: in a way that achieves your aims or what was intended
examples: She had already successfully completed these courses.

meaning: used to show that you are almost certain of what you are saying and want other people to agree with you
examples: Surely we should do something about it?

meaning: used to introduce the logical result of something that has just been mentioned
examples: He's only 17 and therefore not eligible to vote.

meaning: in or into every part of something
examples: They export their products to markets throughout the world.
They come from totally different cultures.

meaning: used to emphasize that a particular statement, feeling, etc. is sincere or real
examples: She truly believes that none of this is her fault.
I don't know him well; I've only met him twice.

meaning: used to say that something usually happens in the way that you are stating
examples: The standard chips are typically used for databases and other business software.
A poor diet will ultimately lead to illness.

meaning: used to say that a particular situation or fact makes you sad or disappointed, or gets you into a difficult position
examples: Unfortunately, I won't be able to attend the meeting.

meaning: towards a higher place or position
examples: Place your hands on the table with the palms facing upwards.

meaning: used in questions to ask the reason for or purpose of something
by a lot of people; in or to many places
examples: The term is widely used in everyday speech.
